security_deposit.allowed_deductions_rule: - Permissible deductions: Unpaid rent, damage beyond normal wear and tear.
security_deposit.deposit_storage_segregation_rule: - Separate account: Not required by state law.
security_deposit.special_rules_local_overrides_note: Local ordinances and special housing programs can add stricter rules.

eviction.nonpayment_notice_type: - Nonpayment of rent: Most jurisdictions do NOT require pre-litigation notice — landlord may file immediately. Some require a 5-day notice to quit.
eviction.lease_violation_notice_type: - Lease violation: Landlord must give tenant notice and opportunity to cure. Time period depends on the nature of the violation.
eviction.holdover_notice_type: - Month-to-month termination: 30 days written notice required.
eviction.timeline_estimate_rule: - Eviction timeline: Uncontested cases approximately 2-4 weeks.
eviction.service_of_process_rule: - Court procedure: Landlord files suit in associate circuit court; tenant is served; court hearing scheduled within days.

landlord_entry.allowed_entry_reasons_rule: - Entry for repairs: Landlord may enter to make repairs, inspections, or to supply necessary services. - Entry to show: Landlord may enter to show the premises to prospective tenants during the last 30 days of tenancy.
landlord_entry.entry_time_window_rule: - Entry times: Entry should be at reasonable times during normal business hours.
landlord_entry.penalty_for_illegal_entry_rule: Varies — confirm remedies (injunction, damages, attorney fees) under state law.

rent_increase.fixed_term_increase_allowed_rule: - Fixed-term leases: Rent cannot be increased during the lease term.
rent_increase.month_to_month_increase_allowed_rule: - Rent increases: No statutory limit on amount or frequency for month-to-month tenancies.
rent_increase.delivery_method_rule: - Notice required: For month-to-month tenancies, at least 30 days advance written notice.
rent_increase.retaliation_protection_note: Retaliation protections may limit rent increases after protected tenant complaints; confirm local/state rules.

habitability.landlord_repair_duty_rule: - Landlord's duty: Maintain structural components, plumbing, heating, electrical systems; provide running water and hot water; comply with housing codes.
habitability.tenant_termination_for_unrepaired_condit: 3. Termination: Tenant may terminate the rental agreement if conditions substantially impair health or safety.
habitability.code_enforcement_contact_rule: Varies — contact local housing/code enforcement (city/county) for inspections and official repair orders.

sources.title: Mo. Rev. Stat. § 441.234; § 441.500 et seq.
extractedQuotes: - Implied warranty of habitability: Missouri recognizes that landlords must maintain rental premises in a fit and habitable condition.

sources.title: Mo. Rev. Stat. § 441.234; § 441.500 et seq.
extractedQuotes: - Landlord's duty: Maintain structural components, plumbing, heating, electrical systems; provide running water and hot water; comply with housing codes.

sources.title: Mo. Rev. Stat. § 441.234; § 441.500 et seq.
extractedQuotes: - Tenant's remedies:

sources.title: Mo. Rev. Stat. § 441.234; § 441.500 et seq.
extractedQuotes: 1. Repair and deduct: ALLOWED. Mo. Rev. Stat. § 441.234: Tenant may have repairs made and deduct up to the lesser of $300 or one-half month's rent, per repair occurrence, after giving landlord proper written notice and reasonable time to repair.

sources.title: Mo. Rev. Stat. § 441.234; § 441.500 et seq.
extractedQuotes: 2. Rent withholding: NOT ALLOWED. Tenant may NOT withhold rent for habitability issues.

sources.title: Mo. Rev. Stat. § 441.234; § 441.500 et seq.
extractedQuotes: 3. Termination: Tenant may terminate the rental agreement if conditions substantially impair health or safety.

sources.title: Mo. Rev. Stat. § 441.234; § 441.500 et seq.
extractedQuotes: - Retaliatory eviction: PROHIBITED. Mo. Rev. Stat. § 441.500 et seq. prohibits eviction in retaliation for tenant's good-faith complaints to government agencies.

sources.title: Mo. Rev. Stat. § 441.234; § 441.500 et seq.
